name: Green Pepper Steak
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tbsp Grated fresh ginger (or 1/2 tsp ground ginger)
1 cup Red/green peppers, cut into 1-inch squares
recipeInstructions:
1. With a sharp knife, cut beef across the grain into thin strips (about 1/8 inch).
2. In a bowl, combine soy sauce, garlic, and grated ginger. Add beef and set aside while preparing vegetables.
3. Heat oil in a large frying pan or wok. Add beef and toss over high heat until browned.
4. Taste the meat; if it is not tender, continue cooking on low heat for 30-40 minutes.
5. Increase heat to medium-high and add vegetables. Toss until vegetables are tender-crisp, about 10 minutes.
6. Mix cornstarch with 1 cup of water in a separate bowl.
7. Add the slurry to the pan, stirring continuously, and cook until thickened.
8. Add tomatoes and heat through.
9. Serve over rice.
Note: You can prepare the beef ahead and refrigerate it. Just before adding vegetables, finish cooking. For 2 servings, use half the ingredients. For 8 servings, double the ingredients but reduce water to 1 3/4 cups and add an additional 1/2 tsp cornstarch.
